findings.evidence: Lines 401-407 define `isoOfLinearEquiv (e) (h) (hinv)`; Mathlib provides tensor equivalence infrastructure (`mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Map.lean:248:def congr`, `:110:theorem map_comp`) sufficient to transport the equation across `e.symm`.
findings.fix: Change `isoOfLinearEquiv` to take only the forward coaction-compatibility proof and derive the inverse compatibility internally, e.g. using `TensorProduct.congr e (LinearEquiv.refl R C)`/`TensorProduct.map_comp`; keep or add a separate lemma for the derived inverse side if useful.
findings.issue: `isoOfLinearEquiv` requires both forward compatibility `h` and inverse compatibility `hinv`, so downstream users must prove duplicate data for an invertible map.

summary: The new isomorphism API exposes an unnecessary proof obligation for the main linear-equivalence constructor.
